Host.description: Restricts the following declarations to hosts matching the pattern. * matches all. Supports wildcards and negation (!).

Match.description: Conditional block matching on host, user, address, etc. More flexible than Host.

HostName.description: The real hostname or IP to connect to. Allows using short aliases in Host.

User.description: The remote username to log in as.

Port.description: The port number to connect to on the remote host. Default is 22.

IdentityFile.description: Path to the private key file. Multiple can be specified; they are tried in order.

ProxyJump.description: Connect through one or more jump hosts (bastion). Replacement for ProxyCommand.

LocalForward.description: Forward a local port through SSH to a destination accessible from the remote host.

RemoteForward.description: Forward a remote port back to the local machine through the SSH tunnel.

DynamicForward.description: Set up a local SOCKS5 proxy forwarding through the SSH connection.

ForwardAgent.description: Forward the SSH authentication agent to the remote machine. Use with caution.

ServerAliveInterval.description: Send a keep-alive message every N seconds if no data has been received. Prevents connection drops.

StrictHostKeyChecking.description: Controls host key verification. accept-new adds unknown keys but rejects changed ones.

AddKeysToAgent.description: Automatically add keys to the running ssh-agent after authentication.

String.description: Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

null.description: Represents the intentional absence of any value.

undefined.description: Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

print.description: Output a value to the standard output stream.
